name: Norm Crosby
blurb: The Master of Malaprop — a one-trick act so meticulously committed that the trick became one of the great running gags in American comedy.
bio: Norm Crosby was a Boston-born ex-Navy man (his hearing damaged in WWII) who built an entire 60-year career on getting words almost right — "a man of great statue," "a hostile of nuns," "the cymbals of our country." The act sounds slight on paper; in performance it was iron-disciplined, because the malaprops had to land at the speed of normal speech, and they had to be the wrong word that you almost believed was the right one. Crosby made his Tonight Show debut in December 1964 and went on to 57 appearances with Carson; from 1974 to 1984 he was a fixture on the Dean Martin Celebrity Roasts, where the malaprop bit cut through the room like a stiletto. He hosted the syndicated The Comedy Shop (1978–81), a stand-up showcase that mixed vaudeville veterans with rising young comics, and headlined Las Vegas and the Borscht Belt for decades.

timeline.ev: First appearance on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson (December)
Co-stars on NBC's The Beautiful Phyllis Diller Show
Begins appearing on the Dean Martin Celebrity Roasts — a run of more than half a dozen through 1984
Hosts the syndicated stand-up showcase The Comedy Shop (through 1981)
Dies November 7 in Los Angeles, age 93

accolades: 57 appearances on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson
Recurring roaster on the Dean Martin Celebrity Roasts (1974–1984)
Host of the syndicated stand-up series The Comedy Shop (1978–1981)
Las Vegas and Borscht Belt headliner across six decades
